instruction: Implement comprehensive Rust integration tests with test fixtures, shared setup, and proper test isolation for a web service with database dependencies.
output:
<think>
Rust integration tests live in the `tests/` directory and each file is compiled as a separate crate. This means they can only access the public API of your library. Key patterns:

1. Shared setup via a common module: `tests/common/mod.rs`
2. Test database with migration and rollback
3. HTTP test client for API testing
4. Test fixtures using builder patterns
5. Cleanup via Drop trait

Rust doesn't have pytest-style fixtures, so we build our own infrastructure. Common patterns:
- TestContext struct that holds DB pool, HTTP client, etc.
- impl Drop for cleanup
- Builder pattern for test data
- #[tokio::test] for async tests
- serial_test crate for tests that can't run in parallel

I need to handle the fact that Rust tests run in parallel by default. For DB tests, each test should use its own database or transaction.
</think>

```rust
// tests/common/mod.rs — Shared test infrastructure

use sqlx::{PgPool, postgres::PgPoolOptions, Executor};
use reqwest::Client;
use std::sync::Once;
use uuid::Uuid;

static INIT: Once = Once::new();

pub struct TestContext {
    pub db: PgPool,
    pub client: Client,
    pub base_url: String,
    db_name: String,
}

impl TestContext {
    pub async fn new() -> Self {
        INIT.call_once(|| {
            // Initialize logging once
            tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();
        });

        // Each test gets its own database
        let db_name = format!("test_{}", Uuid::new_v4().to_string().replace('-', ""));
        let admin_url = std::env::var("DATABASE_URL")
            .unwrap_or_else(|_| "postgres://test:test@localhost/postgres".into());

        // Create test database
        let admin_pool = PgPool::connect(&admin_url).await.unwrap();
        admin_pool
            .execute(format!("CREATE DATABASE {}", db_name).as_str())
            .await
            .unwrap();

        // Connect to test database
        let test_url = admin_url.replace("/postgres", &format!("/{}", db_name));
        let db = PgPoolOptions::new()
            .max_connections(5)
            .connect(&test_url)
            .await
            .unwrap();

        // Run migrations
        sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")
            .run(&db)
            .await
            .unwrap();

        // Start test server
        let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("127.0.0.1:0").await.unwrap();
        let port = listener.local_addr().unwrap().port();
        let base_url = format!("http://127.0.0.1:{}", port);

        // let app = myapp::create_app(db.clone());
        // tokio::spawn(async move { axum::serve(listener, app).await.unwrap() });

        let client = Client::builder()
            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))
            .build()
            .unwrap();

        TestContext { db, client, base_url, db_name }
    }

    pub fn url(&self, path: &str) -> String {
        format!("{}{}", self.base_url, path)
    }
}

impl Drop for TestContext {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        // Drop the test database
        // Note: This runs synchronously in Drop, which is tricky.
        // In practice, use a cleanup script or accept some leaked DBs.
        let db_name = self.db_name.clone();
        let _ = std::thread::spawn(move || {
            let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
            rt.block_on(async {
                let admin_url = std::env::var("DATABASE_URL")
                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| "postgres://test:test@localhost/postgres".into());
                if let Ok(pool) = PgPool::connect(&admin_url).await {
                    let _ = pool
                        .execute(format!("DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS {} WITH (FORCE)", db_name).as_str())
                        .await;
                }
            });
        })
        .join();
    }
}

// --- Test Data Builders ---

pub struct UserBuilder {
    name: String,
    email: String,
    role: String,
}

impl UserBuilder {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        let id = Uuid::new_v4();
        Self {
            name: format!("Test User {}", &id.to_string()[..8]),
            email: format!("test-{}@example.com", &id.to_string()[..8]),
            role: "user".into(),
        }
    }

    pub fn name(mut self, name: &str) -> Self {
        self.name = name.into();
        self
    }

    pub fn email(mut self, email: &str) -> Self {
        self.email = email.into();
        self
    }

    pub fn admin(mut self) -> Self {
        self.role = "admin".into();
        self
    }

    pub async fn build(self, db: &PgPool) -> i64 {
        let row = sqlx::query_scalar!(
            "INSERT INTO users (name, email, role) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) RETURNING id",
            self.name, self.email, self.role
        )
        .fetch_one(db)
        .await
        .unwrap();
        row
    }
}

// tests/api_tests.rs — Integration tests

mod common;
use common::{TestContext, UserBuilder};

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_create_user() {
    let ctx = TestContext::new().await;

    let response = ctx.client
        .post(ctx.url("/api/users"))
        .json(&serde_json::json!({
            "name": "Alice",
            "email": "alice@example.com"
        }))
        .send()
        .await
        .unwrap();

    assert_eq!(response.status(), 201);
    let body: serde_json::Value = response.json().await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(body["name"], "Alice");
    assert!(body["id"].as_i64().unwrap() > 0);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_get_user_not_found() {
    let ctx = TestContext::new().await;

    let response = ctx.client
        .get(ctx.url("/api/users/99999"))
        .send()
        .await
        .unwrap();

    assert_eq!(response.status(), 404);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_list_users_with_fixture() {
    let ctx = TestContext::new().await;

    // Create test data using builder
    let _alice = UserBuilder::new().name("Alice").email("a@test.com").build(&ctx.db).await;
    let _bob = UserBuilder::new().name("Bob").email("b@test.com").admin().build(&ctx.db).await;

    let response = ctx.client
        .get(ctx.url("/api/users"))
        .send()
        .await
        .unwrap();

    assert_eq!(response.status(), 200);
    let body: serde_json::Value = response.json().await.unwrap();
    let users = body.as_array().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(users.len(), 2);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_database_isolation() {
    // Each test gets its own database, so this test sees zero users
    let ctx = TestContext::new().await;

    let count: (i64,) = sqlx::query_as("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users")
        .fetch_one(&ctx.db)
        .await
        .unwrap();

    assert_eq!(count.0, 0); // Clean database!
}
```

instruction: Implement Rust doc tests and proptest-based property testing for a library, including custom strategies, shrinking, and regression file management.
output:
<think>
Rust has two powerful testing approaches beyond unit tests:

1. Doc tests: code examples in documentation comments that are compiled and run as tests. They serve dual purpose — documentation AND tests. If the docs show incorrect usage, the test fails.

2. proptest: Rust's property-based testing framework (like Hypothesis for Python). It generates random inputs satisfying constraints and tries to find counterexamples. When it finds a failure, it shrinks the input to a minimal reproducing case.

Key proptest concepts:
- Strategies: describe how to generate random values
- proptest! macro: defines property tests
- prop_compose!: build complex custom strategies
- Shrinking: automatically reduces failing inputs to minimal cases
- Regression files: persist failing cases so they're re-tested in future runs
- ProptestConfig: control number of cases, timeout, etc.

I'll build a small library (a Money type and a text parser) with comprehensive doc tests and property tests.
</think>

```rust
//! # Money Library
//!
//! A type-safe money representation with proper arithmetic.
//!
//! ## Examples
//!
//! ```
//! use money_lib::{Money, Currency};
//!
//! let price = Money::new(1999, Currency::USD); // $19.99
//! let tax = price.percentage(8.25);
//! let total = price + tax;
//!
//! assert_eq!(total.cents(), 2164); // $21.64
//! assert_eq!(total.to_string(), "$21.64");
//! ```

use std::fmt;
use std::ops::{Add, Mul, Sub};

/// Supported currencies.
///
/// ```
/// use money_lib::Currency;
///
/// let usd = Currency::USD;
/// assert_eq!(usd.symbol(), "$");
/// assert_eq!(usd.code(), "USD");
/// ```
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum Currency {
    USD,
    EUR,
    GBP,
}

impl Currency {
    pub fn symbol(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Currency::USD => "$",
            Currency::EUR => "\u{20ac}",
            Currency::GBP => "\u{00a3}",
        }
    }

    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Currency::USD => "USD",
            Currency::EUR => "EUR",
            Currency::GBP => "GBP",
        }
    }
}

/// Money represented as integer cents to avoid floating-point issues.
///
/// # Construction
///
/// ```
/// use money_lib::{Money, Currency};
///
/// // From cents
/// let m = Money::new(1050, Currency::USD);
/// assert_eq!(m.dollars(), 10);
/// assert_eq!(m.cents_part(), 50);
///
/// // From dollars and cents
/// let m = Money::from_dollars(10, 50, Currency::USD);
/// assert_eq!(m.cents(), 1050);
/// ```
///
/// # Arithmetic
///
/// ```
/// use money_lib::{Money, Currency};
///
/// let a = Money::new(1000, Currency::USD);
/// let b = Money::new(500, Currency::USD);
///
/// assert_eq!((a + b).cents(), 1500);
/// assert_eq!((a - b).cents(), 500);
/// ```
///
/// # Panics on currency mismatch
///
/// ```should_panic
/// use money_lib::{Money, Currency};
///
/// let usd = Money::new(100, Currency::USD);
/// let eur = Money::new(100, Currency::EUR);
/// let _ = usd + eur; // panics!
/// ```
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Money {
    cents: i64,
    currency: Currency,
}

impl Money {
    pub fn new(cents: i64, currency: Currency) -> Self {
        Self { cents, currency }
    }

    pub fn from_dollars(dollars: i64, cents: i64, currency: Currency) -> Self {
        Self {
            cents: dollars * 100 + cents,
            currency,
        }
    }

    pub fn zero(currency: Currency) -> Self {
        Self { cents: 0, currency }
    }

    pub fn cents(&self) -> i64 { self.cents }
    pub fn currency(&self) -> Currency { self.currency }
    pub fn dollars(&self) -> i64 { self.cents / 100 }
    pub fn cents_part(&self) -> i64 { (self.cents % 100).abs() }

    /// Calculate a percentage of this amount (banker's rounding).
    ///
    /// ```
    /// use money_lib::{Money, Currency};
    ///
    /// let price = Money::new(1000, Currency::USD); // $10.00
    /// let tax = price.percentage(10.0);              // 10% = $1.00
    /// assert_eq!(tax.cents(), 100);
    ///
    /// let odd = Money::new(333, Currency::USD);       // $3.33
    /// let half = odd.percentage(50.0);                 // 50% = $1.67 (rounded)
    /// assert_eq!(half.cents(), 167);
    /// ```
    pub fn percentage(&self, pct: f64) -> Self {
        let result = (self.cents as f64 * pct / 100.0).round() as i64;
        Self { cents: result, currency: self.currency }
    }
}

impl Add for Money {
    type Output = Self;
    fn add(self, rhs: Self) -> Self {
        assert_eq!(self.currency, rhs.currency, "Currency mismatch");
        Self { cents: self.cents + rhs.cents, currency: self.currency }
    }
}

impl Sub for Money {
    type Output = Self;
    fn sub(self, rhs: Self) -> Self {
        assert_eq!(self.currency, rhs.currency, "Currency mismatch");
        Self { cents: self.cents - rhs.cents, currency: self.currency }
    }
}

impl fmt::Display for Money {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
        let sign = if self.cents < 0 { "-" } else { "" };
        write!(f, "{}{}{}.{:02}",
            sign, self.currency.symbol(),
            self.dollars().abs(), self.cents_part())
    }
}

// ===== Property Tests =====

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use proptest::prelude::*;

    // Custom strategy for Money
    fn money_strategy() -> impl Strategy<Value = Money> {
        (any::<i64>().prop_map(|c| c % 1_000_000_00), // Reasonable range
         prop_oneof![Just(Currency::USD), Just(Currency::EUR), Just(Currency::GBP)])
        .prop_map(|(cents, currency)| Money::new(cents, currency))
    }

    // Strategy for same-currency money pairs
    fn same_currency_pair() -> impl Strategy<Value = (Money, Money)> {
        prop_oneof![Just(Currency::USD), Just(Currency::EUR), Just(Currency::GBP)]
            .prop_flat_map(|currency| {
                (any::<i32>().prop_map(|c| c as i64),
                 any::<i32>().prop_map(|c| c as i64))
                .prop_map(move |(a, b)| {
                    (Money::new(a, currency), Money::new(b, currency))
                })
            })
    }

    proptest! {
        #![proptest_config(ProptestConfig::with_cases(1000))]

        #[test]
        fn addition_is_commutative((a, b) in same_currency_pair()) {
            prop_assert_eq!(a + b, b + a);
        }

        #[test]
        fn addition_is_associative(
            currency in prop_oneof![Just(Currency::USD), Just(Currency::EUR)],
            ca in any::<i32>().prop_map(|c| c as i64),
            cb in any::<i32>().prop_map(|c| c as i64),
            cc in any::<i32>().prop_map(|c| c as i64),
        ) {
            let a = Money::new(ca, currency);
            let b = Money::new(cb, currency);
            let c = Money::new(cc, currency);
            prop_assert_eq!((a + b) + c, a + (b + c));
        }

        #[test]
        fn add_zero_is_identity(m in money_strategy()) {
            let zero = Money::zero(m.currency());
            prop_assert_eq!(m + zero, m);
            prop_assert_eq!(zero + m, m);
        }

        #[test]
        fn sub_self_is_zero(m in money_strategy()) {
            let result = m - m;
            prop_assert_eq!(result.cents(), 0);
        }

        #[test]
        fn display_roundtrips(cents in -999_999i64..999_999i64) {
            let m = Money::new(cents, Currency::USD);
            let s = m.to_string();
            // Display should always produce valid format
            prop_assert!(s.contains("$"));
            prop_assert!(s.contains("."));
        }

        #[test]
        fn percentage_zero_is_zero(m in money_strategy()) {
            prop_assert_eq!(m.percentage(0.0).cents(), 0);
        }

        #[test]
        fn percentage_hundred_is_identity(cents in -100_000i64..100_000i64) {
            let m = Money::new(cents, Currency::USD);
            prop_assert_eq!(m.percentage(100.0).cents(), cents);
        }
    }

    // Explicit regression test (auto-generated by proptest on failure)
    #[test]
    fn regression_add_overflow() {
        // This would be auto-added to proptest-regressions/ file
        let a = Money::new(i32::MAX as i64, Currency::USD);
        let b = Money::new(1, Currency::USD);
        let result = a + b;
        assert_eq!(result.cents(), i32::MAX as i64 + 1);
    }
}
```

instruction: Implement testing async Rust code with tokio::test, including testing timeouts, task spawning, channel communication, and mock async dependencies.
output:
<think>
Testing async Rust code has specific challenges:
1. Need a runtime — #[tokio::test] provides one
2. Timeouts — tests should fail fast if something hangs
3. Time manipulation — tokio::time::pause() for deterministic time-based tests
4. Mocking async traits — using async-trait + mockall or manual mocks
5. Testing channels and task communication
6. Testing cancellation behavior

Key patterns:
- tokio::time::pause() freezes time, advance() moves it forward deterministically
- tokio::time::timeout() wraps assertions to prevent hangs
- Mock async services using traits + manual implementations
- Test channel-based protocols by simulating both sides
- Use #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)] for time-sensitive tests

I'll show a realistic service with dependencies and comprehensive async tests.
</think>

```rust
use std::time::Duration;
use tokio::sync::{mpsc, oneshot};

// --- Production code ---

#[async_trait::async_trait]
pub trait UserRepository: Send + Sync {
    async fn find_by_id(&self, id: u64) -> Result<User, AppError>;
    async fn save(&self, user: &User) -> Result<(), AppError>;
}

#[async_trait::async_trait]
pub trait NotificationService: Send + Sync {
    async fn send_email(&self, to: &str, subject: &str, body: &str) -> Result<(), AppError>;
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub struct User {
    pub id: u64,
    pub name: String,
    pub email: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum AppError {
    #[error("not found")]
    NotFound,
    #[error("timeout")]
    Timeout,
    #[error("internal: {0}")]
    Internal(String),
}

pub struct UserService<R: UserRepository, N: NotificationService> {
    repo: R,
    notifications: N,
    timeout: Duration,
}

impl<R: UserRepository, N: NotificationService> UserService<R, N> {
    pub fn new(repo: R, notifications: N, timeout: Duration) -> Self {
        Self { repo, notifications, timeout }
    }

    pub async fn get_user(&self, id: u64) -> Result<User, AppError> {
        tokio::time::timeout(self.timeout, self.repo.find_by_id(id))
            .await
            .map_err(|_| AppError::Timeout)?
    }

    pub async fn update_and_notify(&self, user: &User) -> Result<(), AppError> {
        self.repo.save(user).await?;
        // Fire-and-forget notification (don't block on it)
        let email = user.email.clone();
        let name = user.name.clone();
        // In real code, this would be spawned
        let _ = self.notifications
            .send_email(&email, "Profile Updated", &format!("Hi {name}"))
            .await;
        Ok(())
    }
}

// --- Command processor with channels ---

#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum Command {
    GetUser { id: u64, reply: oneshot::Sender<Result<User, AppError>> },
    Shutdown,
}

pub async fn command_processor(
    mut rx: mpsc::Receiver<Command>,
    repo: impl UserRepository,
) {
    while let Some(cmd) = rx.recv().await {
        match cmd {
            Command::GetUser { id, reply } => {
                let result = repo.find_by_id(id).await;
                let _ = reply.send(result);
            }
            Command::Shutdown => break,
        }
    }
}

// ===== Tests =====

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};

    // --- Mock implementations ---

    struct MockRepo {
        users: Vec<User>,
        save_calls: Arc<Mutex<Vec<User>>>,
        delay: Option<Duration>,
    }

    impl MockRepo {
        fn new(users: Vec<User>) -> Self {
            Self {
                users,
                save_calls: Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new())),
                delay: None,
            }
        }

        fn with_delay(mut self, d: Duration) -> Self {
            self.delay = Some(d);
            self
        }

        fn saved(&self) -> Vec<User> {
            self.save_calls.lock().unwrap().clone()
        }
    }

    #[async_trait::async_trait]
    impl UserRepository for MockRepo {
        async fn find_by_id(&self, id: u64) -> Result<User, AppError> {
            if let Some(delay) = self.delay {
                tokio::time::sleep(delay).await;
            }
            self.users.iter()
                .find(|u| u.id == id)
                .cloned()
                .ok_or(AppError::NotFound)
        }

        async fn save(&self, user: &User) -> Result<(), AppError> {
            self.save_calls.lock().unwrap().push(user.clone());
            Ok(())
        }
    }

    struct MockNotifications {
        sent: Arc<Mutex<Vec<(String, String, String)>>>,
    }

    impl MockNotifications {
        fn new() -> Self {
            Self { sent: Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new())) }
        }

        fn sent_emails(&self) -> Vec<(String, String, String)> {
            self.sent.lock().unwrap().clone()
        }
    }

    #[async_trait::async_trait]
    impl NotificationService for MockNotifications {
        async fn send_email(&self, to: &str, subject: &str, body: &str) -> Result<(), AppError> {
            self.sent.lock().unwrap().push((
                to.to_string(), subject.to_string(), body.to_string()
            ));
            Ok(())
        }
    }

    fn test_user() -> User {
        User { id: 1, name: "Alice".into(), email: "alice@test.com".into() }
    }

    // --- Tests ---

    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_get_user_success() {
        let repo = MockRepo::new(vec![test_user()]);
        let notif = MockNotifications::new();
        let svc = UserService::new(repo, notif, Duration::from_secs(5));

        let user = svc.get_user(1).await.unwrap();
        assert_eq!(user.name, "Alice");
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_get_user_not_found() {
        let repo = MockRepo::new(vec![]);
        let notif = MockNotifications::new();
        let svc = UserService::new(repo, notif, Duration::from_secs(5));

        let err = svc.get_user(999).await.unwrap_err();
        assert!(matches!(err, AppError::NotFound));
    }

    #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)]  // Deterministic time!
    async fn test_get_user_timeout() {
        let repo = MockRepo::new(vec![test_user()])
            .with_delay(Duration::from_secs(10)); // Slow repo
        let notif = MockNotifications::new();
        let svc = UserService::new(repo, notif, Duration::from_secs(2)); // 2s timeout

        let err = svc.get_user(1).await.unwrap_err();
        assert!(matches!(err, AppError::Timeout));
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_update_sends_notification() {
        let repo = MockRepo::new(vec![]);
        let notif = MockNotifications::new();
        let svc = UserService::new(repo, notif, Duration::from_secs(5));

        let user = test_user();
        svc.update_and_notify(&user).await.unwrap();

        let emails = svc.notifications.sent_emails();
        assert_eq!(emails.len(), 1);
        assert_eq!(emails[0].0, "alice@test.com");
        assert!(emails[0].2.contains("Alice"));
    }

    // --- Channel-based tests ---

    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_command_processor() {
        let repo = MockRepo::new(vec![test_user()]);
        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(32);

        let handle = tokio::spawn(command_processor(rx, repo));

        // Send command and get response
        let (reply_tx, reply_rx) = oneshot::channel();
        tx.send(Command::GetUser { id: 1, reply: reply_tx }).await.unwrap();

        let user = reply_rx.await.unwrap().unwrap();
        assert_eq!(user.name, "Alice");

        // Shutdown
        tx.send(Command::Shutdown).await.unwrap();
        handle.await.unwrap();
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_command_processor_handles_not_found() {
        let repo = MockRepo::new(vec![]);
        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(32);

        tokio::spawn(command_processor(rx, repo));

        let (reply_tx, reply_rx) = oneshot::channel();
        tx.send(Command::GetUser { id: 999, reply: reply_tx }).await.unwrap();

        let result = reply_rx.await.unwrap();
        assert!(matches!(result, Err(AppError::NotFound)));
    }

    // Test with timeout to prevent hangs
    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_does_not_hang() {
        let result = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), async {
            let repo = MockRepo::new(vec![test_user()]);
            let notif = MockNotifications::new();
            let svc = UserService::new(repo, notif, Duration::from_secs(5));
            svc.get_user(1).await
        })
        .await
        .expect("Test timed out")
        .expect("Get user failed");

        assert_eq!(result.id, 1);
    }
}
```

instruction: Implement Rust proptest strategies for testing a text parser, including custom string strategies, regex-based generation, and testing parser round-trip properties.
output:
<think>
Property-based testing for parsers is one of the most valuable applications. The key property: if you can parse a string into a structured type and then format it back, the result should be parseable again (round-trip property).

For proptest, I need:
1. Custom strategies that generate valid inputs matching the grammar
2. Regex-based strategies for pattern-based fields
3. Composite strategies using prop_compose!
4. Round-trip property tests
5. Error case generation (invalid inputs)

I'll test a simple DSN/URL parser since it has enough complexity to show interesting strategies: scheme, user:pass, host, port, path, query params.

The round-trip property: parse(format(parsed)) == parsed. This is extremely powerful — it finds bugs in both the parser and the formatter.

I also need strategies that generate INVALID inputs to test error handling. These use broader string generation that includes edge cases the custom strategies wouldn't produce.
</think>

```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::fmt;

// --- Parser/Formatter under test ---

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct DatabaseUrl {
    pub scheme: String,
    pub username: Option<String>,
    pub password: Option<String>,
    pub host: String,
    pub port: Option<u16>,
    pub database: String,
    pub params: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl DatabaseUrl {
    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, ParseError> {
        let (scheme, rest) = s.split_once("://")
            .ok_or(ParseError::MissingScheme)?;

        let (auth_host, path_params) = rest.split_once('/')
            .ok_or(ParseError::MissingDatabase)?;

        let (userinfo, hostport) = if auth_host.contains('@') {
            let (u, h) = auth_host.rsplit_once('@').unwrap();
            (Some(u), h)
        } else {
            (None, auth_host)
        };

        let (username, password) = match userinfo {
            Some(info) if info.contains(':') => {
                let (u, p) = info.split_once(':').unwrap();
                (Some(u.to_string()), Some(p.to_string()))
            }
            Some(info) => (Some(info.to_string()), None),
            None => (None, None),
        };

        let (host, port) = if hostport.contains(':') {
            let (h, p) = hostport.rsplit_once(':').unwrap();
            let port = p.parse::<u16>().map_err(|_| ParseError::InvalidPort)?;
            (h.to_string(), Some(port))
        } else {
            (hostport.to_string(), None)
        };

        let (database, params) = if path_params.contains('?') {
            let (db, qs) = path_params.split_once('?').unwrap();
            let params: HashMap<String, String> = qs.split('&')
                .filter_map(|pair| pair.split_once('='))
                .map(|(k, v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string()))
                .collect();
            (db.to_string(), params)
        } else {
            (path_params.to_string(), HashMap::new())
        };

        if host.is_empty() { return Err(ParseError::InvalidHost); }
        if database.is_empty() { return Err(ParseError::MissingDatabase); }

        Ok(DatabaseUrl { scheme: scheme.to_string(), username, password, host, port, database, params })
    }
}

impl fmt::Display for DatabaseUrl {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "{}://", self.scheme)?;
        if let Some(ref user) = self.username {
            write!(f, "{}", user)?;
            if let Some(ref pass) = self.password {
                write!(f, ":{}", pass)?;
            }
            write!(f, "@")?;
        }
        write!(f, "{}", self.host)?;
        if let Some(port) = self.port {
            write!(f, ":{}", port)?;
        }
        write!(f, "/{}", self.database)?;
        if !self.params.is_empty() {
            let mut params: Vec<_> = self.params.iter().collect();
            params.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| k.clone());
            write!(f, "?")?;
            for (i, (k, v)) in params.iter().enumerate() {
                if i > 0 { write!(f, "&")?; }
                write!(f, "{}={}", k, v)?;
            }
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
pub enum ParseError {
    MissingScheme,
    MissingDatabase,
    InvalidPort,
    InvalidHost,
}

// ===== Property Tests =====

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use proptest::prelude::*;
    use proptest::collection::hash_map;

    // Strategy for valid scheme names
    fn scheme_strategy() -> impl Strategy<Value = String> {
        prop_oneof![
            Just("postgres".to_string()),
            Just("mysql".to_string()),
            Just("mongodb".to_string()),
            Just("redis".to_string()),
            "[a-z]{3,10}".prop_map(|s| s),
        ]
    }

    // Strategy for hostnames (no special chars that break parsing)
    fn host_strategy() -> impl Strategy<Value = String> {
        prop_oneof![
            Just("localhost".to_string()),
            Just("127.0.0.1".to_string()),
            "[a-z]{1,10}(\\.[a-z]{1,10}){0,3}".prop_map(|s| s),
        ]
    }

    // Strategy for identifiers (usernames, passwords, db names)
    fn ident_strategy() -> impl Strategy<Value = String> {
        "[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,15}".prop_map(|s| s)
    }

    // Strategy for query parameter keys/values
    fn param_strategy() -> impl Strategy<Value = HashMap<String, String>> {
        hash_map(
            "[a-z_]{1,10}".prop_map(|s| s),
            "[a-zA-Z0-9_]{1,20}".prop_map(|s| s),
            0..5,
        )
    }

    // Composite strategy for valid DatabaseUrl
    prop_compose! {
        fn database_url_strategy()(
            scheme in scheme_strategy(),
            username in proptest::option::of(ident_strategy()),
            password in proptest::option::of(ident_strategy()),
            host in host_strategy(),
            port in proptest::option::of(1024u16..65535),
            database in ident_strategy(),
            params in param_strategy(),
        ) -> DatabaseUrl {
            // Password requires username
            let (username, password) = if username.is_some() {
                (username, password)
            } else {
                (None, None)
            };
            DatabaseUrl { scheme, username, password, host, port, database, params }
        }
    }

    proptest! {
        #![proptest_config(ProptestConfig::with_cases(500))]

        /// ROUND-TRIP: format then parse should yield the original
        #[test]
        fn roundtrip_format_parse(url in database_url_strategy()) {
            let formatted = url.to_string();
            let parsed = DatabaseUrl::parse(&formatted)
                .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("Failed to parse {:?}: {:?}", formatted, e));
            prop_assert_eq!(&parsed, &url,
                "Round-trip failed for: {}", formatted);
        }

        /// ROUND-TRIP: parse then format then parse should be stable
        #[test]
        fn roundtrip_parse_format_parse(url in database_url_strategy()) {
            let s1 = url.to_string();
            let p1 = DatabaseUrl::parse(&s1).unwrap();
            let s2 = p1.to_string();
            let p2 = DatabaseUrl::parse(&s2).unwrap();
            prop_assert_eq!(p1, p2);
        }

        /// Parsed scheme always matches the prefix
        #[test]
        fn scheme_is_preserved(url in database_url_strategy()) {
            let formatted = url.to_string();
            prop_assert!(formatted.starts_with(&format!("{}://", url.scheme)));
        }

        /// Port is always in valid range
        #[test]
        fn port_in_range(url in database_url_strategy()) {
            if let Some(port) = url.port {
                prop_assert!(port > 0);
            }
        }

        /// Random strings shouldn't crash the parser
        #[test]
        fn parser_never_panics(s in ".*") {
            let _ = DatabaseUrl::parse(&s); // May return Err, but must not panic
        }

        /// Strings without :// always fail with MissingScheme
        #[test]
        fn no_scheme_separator_fails(s in "[^:]*") {
            prop_assert_eq!(
                DatabaseUrl::parse(&s).unwrap_err(),
                ParseError::MissingScheme
            );
        }
    }
}
```
